title: Just Pay the Minimum?
scenario: Your credit card bill is ₹30,000 this month. The bank says you can just pay the 'minimum due' and carry the rest. The interest rate is around 36% a year. What do you do?

choices.label: Pay the minimum, carry the balance
choices.lesson: Credit card 'minimum due' is a debt trap: ~36% annual interest compounds fast and can balloon a small bill into a long-term burden.

choices.label: Pay the bill in full
choices.lesson: Used well, a credit card is free short-term credit. The rule is simple: always pay the full statement, never just the minimum.
